headline: Finite-density QCD modeling, baryon-stopping observables, hot-QCD screening masses, and analysis-grade ML validation were the most useful themes in the June 26 scout.
summary: The June 26 listing is strongest for finite-density heavy-ion infrastructure and diagnostics: MUSES/Calliope equation-of-state workflows, rapidity-even directed-flow splitting for baryon stopping, and STAR intermittency reinterpretation. Hot-QCD screening masses provide a strong theory item, while the AI/ML selections emphasize interpretability/explainability discipline and nuisance-aware unfolding rather than generic model performance.
strongest_paper: Studying the QCD Matter produced in Heavy-Ion Collisions using the MUSES Calculation Engine
top_takeaway: The strongest same-day contribution is reproducible finite-density heavy-ion infrastructure, while the most immediately data-facing physics themes are baryon stopping, intermittency, and jet-medium structure.

papers.title: Applicability of kinetic theory in strongly coupled thermal quantum systems
papers.authors: Shile Chen; Shuzhe Shi; Pengfei Zhuang
papers.abstract: Finite-temperature momentum-distribution study in simplified strongly interacting spinor systems, used to diagnose when two-particle correlations become subleading enough for kinetic theory.

papers.title: Axial-Vector Lattice Benchmarks Reveal a Common Medium Response of Meson Screening in Hot QCD
papers.authors: Jie Ren; Chen Chen; Fei Gao; Si-xue Qin
papers.abstract: Combines lattice-QCD screening-mass benchmarks with a Dyson-Schwinger baseline to model flavor-dependent meson screening in hot QCD.

papers.title: Modification of jet-energy flow in heavy-ion collisions
papers.abstract: ALICE reports jet-energy-flow modification in Pb-Pb relative to pp, constraining radial redistribution of quenched jet energy.

papers.title: Studying the QCD Matter produced in Heavy-Ion Collisions using the MUSES Calculation Engine
papers.authors: Johannes Jahan; Kevin P. Pala; Yumu Yang; Isabella Danhoni; Prachi Garella; et al.
papers.abstract: Presents Calliope, a MUSES calculation engine for finite-density heavy-ion equation-of-state workflows, hydrodynamic inputs, transport-coefficient interfaces, and BES-energy demonstrations.

papers.title: Rapidity-even directed flow splitting of protons and antiprotons as a probe of baryon stopping in relativistic heavy-ion collisions
papers.abstract: Proposes proton-antiproton rapidity-even directed-flow splitting, especially midrapidity curvature, as a discriminator of baryon stopping and diffusion at BES energies.

papers.title: Understanding the Intermittency Signal in RHIC-STAR Data through Modeling
papers.authors: Jin Wu; Zhiming Li; Mingmei Xu; Shuyun Yang; Ranran Guo; Yuanfang Wu
papers.abstract: Uses improved UrQMD+CMC modeling to argue that STAR BES-I intermittency data allow only small, nearly energy-independent critical-like fractions.

papers.title: Mapping jet substructure in heavy-ion collisions with track functions
papers.authors: Joao Barata; Yeonju Go; Daniel Pablos
papers.abstract: Studies track-function moments and cumulants in JEWEL and HYBRID, proposing them as discriminants of microscopic jet-medium dynamics.

papers.title: Ultra-Peripheral Collisions as a Nuclear-Structure Interferometer with Interpretable Multitask Deep Learning
papers.authors: Jing-Zong Zhang; Wang-Mei Zha; Lingxiao Wang; Guo-Liang Ma
papers.abstract: Uses interpretable multitask deep learning to infer nuclear-structure indicators from UPC coherent J/psi photoproduction spectra.

papers.title: Interpreting "Interpretability" and Explaining "Explainability" in Machine Learning in Physics
papers.authors: Rikab Gambhir; Luisa Lucie-Smith; Jesse Thaler
papers.abstract: Conceptual review distinguishing structural interpretability from domain-grounded explainability and emphasizing task-specific intervention plans.

papers.title: Machine Learning-based Unfolding for Cross Section Measurements in the Presence of Nuisance Parameters
papers.authors: Huanbiao Zhu; Krish Desai; Mikael Kuusela; Vinicius Mikuni; Benjamin Nachman; Larry Wasserman
papers.abstract: Introduces Profile OmniFold, extending ML-based unfolding to include nuisance parameters, with a simulated CMS demonstration.

papers.title: Thermodynamical analysis of QGP using effective PNJL model with Quasiparticle approach
papers.authors: Yogesh Kumar; Himanshu Aggarwal; R. S. Laishram; Poonam Jain; Om Prakash; et al.
papers.abstract: Extends a two-flavor PNJL model with quasiparticle masses and reports improved agreement with lattice-QCD thermodynamics.

papers.title: Mass-Dependent Non-Extensivity in Tsallis Blast-Wave Fits to Identified Hadron pT Spectra at RHIC and LHC
papers.abstract: Improves Tsallis Blast-Wave fit quality by allowing the non-extensivity parameter to vary by particle mass or class.
